meta.title: Revolution Square | Plaza de la Revolución
meta.description: Explore Revolution Square in Havana, Cuba. This is Cuba's most important political and cultural landmark, carrying rich historical significance and revolutionary spirit.

hero.title: Revolution Square
hero.subtitle: Plaza de la Revolución · Havana, Cuba

intro.title: Explore Revolution Square
intro.description: Revolution Square (Plaza de la Revolución) is located in Havana, the capital of Cuba. It is one of Cuba's largest and most famous city squares. The square is world-renowned for the José Martí Memorial and the giant wall portraits of Che Guevara and Camilo Cienfuegos. It is not only an important venue for political gatherings in Cuba but also a must-visit place to experience Cuban revolutionary history and cultural spirit.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: The square is open all day, daytime visits are recommended
Visit the José Martí Memorial and Museum
The observation deck offers a panoramic view of Havana

alsoKnownAs.title: Highlights
items: José Martí Memorial: The 109-meter tall memorial is a landmark of Havana, with a museum and observation deck inside.
Che Guevara Portrait: The giant Che Guevara portrait on the Ministry of Interior building is one of Cuba's most iconic images.
Camilo Cienfuegos Portrait: Located on the Ministry of Communications building, it complements the Che Guevara portrait.
Historical Significance: This is the venue for many important political rallies and celebrations in Cuba.

route.title: Tour Route
route.overview: The ideal route to explore Revolution Square:
steps: Start from the center of the square and visit the José Martí Memorial.
Enter the museum inside the memorial to learn about the history of Cuba's independence hero.
Take the elevator or climb the stairs to the observation deck for a panoramic view of Havana.
Visit the surrounding Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Communications buildings to admire the giant portraits of Che and Camilo.
supplements: Morning or evening visits are recommended for the best lighting for photos.
There is an entrance fee for the memorial interior, check opening hours in advance.
The square is large, comfortable walking shoes are recommended.

knowledge.title: Discover the Charm of Revolution Square

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.content: Revolution Square is one of the most important landmarks in modern Cuban history. Since the 1959 Cuban Revolution, it has been the center for major political rallies and celebrations. Every year on May 1st (International Workers' Day) and July 26th (Moncada Barracks Attack anniversary), hundreds of thousands of people gather here.

sections.title: Cultural Symbol
sections.content: The Che Guevara and Camilo Cienfuegos portraits on the square have become global symbols of the Cuban revolutionary spirit. The José Martí Memorial honors Cuba's independence hero and spiritual leader, embodying the Cuban people's sense of pride.

sections.title: Visiting Experience
sections.content: Standing on Revolution Square, you can feel the weight of Cuban history and the passion of the revolution. Overlooking Havana from the memorial observation deck, the beautiful cityscape unfolds before your eyes. This is an unforgettable cultural and historical experience.

hours.title: Opening Hours
hours.warningTime: Recommended to verify latest information before visiting
hours.tip: Daytime visits offer the best experience

tickets.title: Ticket Information
tickets.outdoorPrice: Museum and observation deck require tickets, please inquire on-site for prices
tickets.adultsPrice: Free and open to the public
tickets.studentsPrice: Inquire on-site if guided tours are available
tickets.cardPrice: Bring sun protection and water, the square area has strong sunlight exposure.
tickets.officialNotice: For the latest updates on ticket prices and opening hours, please refer to the
tickets.officialNoticeLinkLabel: Official Cuba Ministry of Tourism Information

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: Approximately 18 km from José Martí International Airport (HAV). Take a taxi or airport bus to the city center, then transfer to Revolution Square.
transport.selfDriveDesc: The square is located in central Havana with convenient transportation, but parking nearby can be difficult. Taxis or public transport are recommended.
transport.busDesc: You can take Havana's buses or taxis. The square is a major city landmark and all drivers know the location.
transport.intercityDesc: The square itself can be explored on foot, and there are many historical buildings worth visiting in the surrounding area.
transport.cyclingDesc: Havana has taxis and bike-sharing services, choose according to your needs.
transport.tipsDesc: Revolution Square is an important place to understand modern Cuban history and revolutionary culture. Daytime visits are recommended, with sufficient time to visit the interior of the memorial.

gallery.subtitle: The Charm of Revolution Square

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Review information can be viewed via Google Maps (external link).
reviews.moreReviews: View More Reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Carlos Rodríguez
items.text: A place full of historical significance. Standing on the square, you can feel the great spirit of the Cuban Revolution and the weight of history.

items.name: María Fernández
items.text: Amazing historical landmark. The José Martí Memorial is spectacular, and you can overlook the beautiful scenery of all Havana from the observation deck.

items.name: John Smith
items.text: Very meaningful place to gain deep understanding of Cuban history and culture. The Che Guevara portrait is impressive.

items.name: Ana López
items.text: An important part of Cuban history. The atmosphere of the square is moving, a must-visit place in Havana.

items.name: Roberto Martínez
items.text: Great historical and cultural experience. The memorial architecture is wonderful, and there are many places worth exploring nearby.

items.name: Sophie Dubois
items.text: Beautiful and solemn square, full of the charm of Cuban history. The Che Guevara portrait is unforgettable, definitely worth a visit.

items.name: Luis Gómez
items.text: Interesting historical venue. Experience Cuban revolutionary history up close, guided tours are recommended for deeper understanding.

items.name: Elena Castro
items.text: An important landmark of Havana. Revolution Square is an important place to understand modern Cuban history, definitely worth experiencing.

officialManagement.title: About Revolution Square
officialManagement.text: Revolution Square is the most important historical landmark and political cultural center in Havana, Cuba, carrying the glorious history of the Cuban Revolution and national spirit. It is a must-visit place for every tourist visiting Havana.

historyTimeline.title: Historical Timeline

items.title: Early 20th Century: Square Construction
items.description: The square was originally named 'Plaza Cívica' (Civic Square) and was built in the early 20th century as an important part of urban planning.

items.title: 1950s: Memorial Construction
items.description: Construction of the José Martí Memorial began in 1953 and was completed in 1976. At 109 meters tall, it is one of the tallest buildings in Havana.

items.title: 1960s: Revolutionary Symbol
items.description: After the revolution, giant Che Guevara portraits were painted on the Ministry of Interior building, and Camilo Cienfuegos portraits on the Ministry of Communications building, making the square a symbol of the Cuban Revolution.

items.title: Modern Era: Renamed to Revolution Square
items.description: The square was renamed 'Plaza de la Revolución' after the 1959 Cuban Revolution, becoming the most important venue for political rallies and cultural events in Cuba.

historyTimeline.guideContent: When visiting, you can start from the center of the square and visit the José Martí Memorial, then ascend to the observation deck for a panoramic view of Havana; then visit the museum inside the memorial to learn about the life and thoughts of Cuba's independence hero José Martí; then head to the surroundings of the square to admire the giant portraits of Che Guevara and Camilo Cienfuegos on the Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Communications buildings. Finally, stroll on the square and feel the solemn atmosphere of this important landmark that carries modern Cuban history.
